answers.where: Yellow Backrooms corridor facing a larger-inside impossible-room aperture with thicker native boxed floor/ceiling/side construction visible inside the portal
answers.action: Use the copied mark as evidence, find the missing handle, and test the locked door
answers.response: The portal room now has additional opaque native box geometry: floor curbs, side ceiling returns, and a central light trough in the non-blended pass
answers.reward: The player sees that the room is not a flat wall decal: it has physical depth that can later be stabilized by fitting the found handle
answers.game_look: Native 3D liminal horror view with a larger-inside portal room, generated Backrooms materials, physical aperture trim, boxed interior construction, shadowed interior, minimal journal UI, and visible wall/floor/ceiling surfaces

issues.text: The new boxed native geometry adds real construction depth, but the aperture still reads partly as a dark hybrid matte/composite instead of a fully production-grade native room or render-target portal.
The non-Euclidean hook is clearer, but the visual signature still is not strong enough to stand apart from high-quality Backrooms games.

problem: The opaque native interior is stronger, with boxed floor and ceiling construction, but the result still depends on a dark fullscreen portal backing and does not yet reach production-quality integrated room lighting/materials.
next: Either replace more of the portal interior with fully native opaque authored surfaces or resolve T0011 render-target-backed portal lighting; keep content expansion frozen while art_quality and audience_fit remain 3.
